summary: The validation set contains 3,597 spectra of 500 unique InChIKeys. The test set contains 3,601 spectra of 500 unique InChIKeys.
MS2DeepScore can predict Tanimoto scores between 0.1 and 0.9 with a RMSE between 0.13 and 0.2.

MS2DeepScore achieves a root mean squared error for predicted Tanimoto scores of about 0.15 when run without uncertainty restrictions. MS2DeepScore achieves a root mean squared error down to 0.1 with stronger restrictions on model uncertain
MS2DeepScore clearly outperforms both classical measures and Spec2Vec with respect to identifying high Tanimoto pairs.
With IQR threshold less than 0.025, the average RMSE drops from about 0.17 to about 0.11.
Predicting all possible similarity scores between 3,601 test spectra (6,485,401 unique pairs) took 5-10 minutes on an Intel i7-8550U CPU.
MS2DeepScore achieves a mean absolute error of about 0.12.
MS2DeepScore uses Tanimoto scores on RDKit Daylight fingerprints with 2048 bits to compute structural similarities.
MS2DeepScore is implemented as a Siamese neural network with two components: a base network and a head part with cosine calculation.
For final test evaluation, all possible spectrum pairs between the 3,601 test spectra were used, totaling 6,485,401 unique pairs.
Tanimoto scores on RDKit Daylight fingerprints result in 10-100 times more pairs in the 0.2-0.9 range compared to Morgan-3 fingerprints.
